world.headline: Trump: 'Honored to meet' Khamenei for Iran deal

main.text: BREAKING: President Trump says he would be honored to meet Iranian Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei if it was to make a deal. First ever face-to-face.

perspectives.text: Negotiating from strength. Pressure then deal.
perspectives.note: The post accurately reports Trump's statement from the video clip (primary source embedded in related coverage). Neutral 'JUST IN' framing with photos; no editorializing in the main text. Lacks caveats on feasibility or prior US actions. High verifiability, primary proximity.

perspectives.text: Double standards after strikes and family deaths.
perspectives.note: Post accurately quotes Trump from the Acyn video (primary). 'Double standards' is opinion framing assuming hypocrisy; partisan loaded language without policy context or caveats on different stated goals vs prior admins. Medium accuracy on quote, lower on neutrality.

world.headline: Dual citizen sent 250 tons tech to Iran nuclear from $35M mansion

main.text: BREAKING: Jamshid Ghomi. Dual citizen. $35M mansion. 250 tons of tech sent to Iran nuclear program. Claimed poverty. FBI taking the mansion.

perspectives.text: America gave him everything. Gave secrets to enemies.
perspectives.note: Official US Attorney statement (primary source) detailing arrest for export violations, dual citizenship, asset forfeiture based on govt investigation. 'Aided our enemy' aligns with charges. Strong on verifiability, accuracy, primary proximity. 'Should be taking the passport' is opinion call for additional action.

perspectives.text: How did this dual citizen operate so long unchecked?
perspectives.note: Points to vetting and security lapses.

world.headline: Trump: After Iran, US will take care of Cuba and remove regime

main.text: JUST IN: President Trump says after dealing with Iran, the US will take care of Cuba and remove its regime.

perspectives.text: Strength: Iran then next Cuba. America First.
perspectives.note: Direct on sequential pressure on adversaries.

perspectives.text: Endless expansion and hunger for regional influence.
perspectives.note: Critical of US foreign policy direction.

nation.headline: Mike Lee offers SAVE America Act on Senate floor

main.text: I’m about to offer the House-passed version of the SAVE America Act on the Senate floor. This one should get at least 51 votes.

perspectives.text: Thank you for pushing SAVE America Act.
perspectives.note: Direct push for election integrity bill.

perspectives.text: Under full GOP control Senate can't pass SAVE.
perspectives.note: Critical of GOP Senate priorities and inaction.

nation.headline: Dem-led block of Trump Pulte as DNI fails 49-49

main.text: A Democrat-led measure in the Senate just FAILED that would have BLOCKED President Trump from naming Bill Pulte as acting Director of National Intelligence, 49-49.

perspectives.text: TRUMP WINS! RINOs sided with Dems to block Pulte.
perspectives.note: Post celebrates the 49-49 failure to block as 'TRUMP WINS'. The vote tally is accurate per the primary EricLDaugh post/video of Senate action. 'RINOs' framing is loaded partisan label (names in main post). Low on neutrality, but fact of vote outcome verifiable.

perspectives.text: Pulte unqualified, plan to interfere in midterms.
perspectives.note: Critical of pick lacking nat sec expertise.

nation.headline: Over half Trump ballroom donors won $50B+ contracts

main.text: BREAKING: More than half of the publicly identified donors to President Trump’s White House ballroom project have won new or expanded federal contracts worth more than $50 billion, per WaPo.

perspectives.text: Insanely corrupt. Pay-to-play with cases dropped.
perspectives.note: Post summarizes WaPo/govt watchdog report on donor contracts and dropped enforcement cases; references judge ruling on construction. Facts attributed to report (verifiable if report accurate). 'Insanely corrupt', 'pay-to-play' is strong loaded framing/advocacy without caveats (e.g. legal lobbying vs illegal). Good source citation, lower neutrality.

perspectives.text: Better look into Obama before you talk shit.
perspectives.note: Points to similar practices in prior administrations.

business.headline: SpaceX will not be fast-tracked into the S&P 500

main.text: SpaceX will reportedly not be fast-tracked into the S&P 500, per the S&P DJI

perspectives.text: S&P rejects fast track for mega-caps like SpaceX.
perspectives.note: Direct on index eligibility rules consistency.

perspectives.text: Great: SpaceX stands on own feet, no special rules.

business.headline: Anthropic starts IPO prep; OpenAI likely by July end

main.text: Anthropic also starting IPO prep. Once decided moves fast. OpenAI and Anthropic IPOs probably by end of July.

perspectives.text: Anthropic IPO prep; OpenAI likely by July.
perspectives.note: Market signal on fast moving AI IPO timelines.

perspectives.text: Huge funds involved to prevent AI valuation bubble.
perspectives.note: Caution on AI valuation bubble during IPO wave.

business.headline: S&P rejects fast track for SpaceX; rules stand

main.text: JUST IN: S&P Dow Jones will not change rules to make it easier for SpaceX to get fast track entry into the S&P 500.

perspectives.text: S&P Dow Jones rejects MegaCap fast track changes.
perspectives.note: Preserves core index principles for representativeness.

perspectives.text: Bureaucracy holds back America's most innovative firm.
perspectives.note: Critique of slow index inclusion process for SpaceX.
